title: Knäckebröd
subtitle: Swedish rye crispbread, thin and seeded
excerpt: Swedish crispbread made from rye flour and seeds, rolled paper-thin and baked until dry and crisp through. No yeast, no bulk ferment — just rye, water, and a hot oven. Good for three months in a tin.
sourceNotes: Knäckebröd has been produced in Scandinavia for centuries, with documented production in Sweden from at least the seventeenth century. The thin-rolled, twice-baked form that became a staple of Swedish military and peasant provisioning is recorded in agricultural and domestic literature from the eighteenth century onward. The recipe here follows the unleavened tradition documented in Swedish domestic handbooks of the nineteenth century: rye flour, water, salt, with seeds added by the baker's choice. The round central-hole form (designed originally to hang on a pole to dry near the rafters) is the traditional domestic shape.

recipe.batchNotes: The recipe doubles easily; bake in batches on two trays.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Store in an airtight tin. Knäckebröd keeps for up to three months and improves in crispness as it dries.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 200°C. The high heat dries and crisps the thin crispbread quickly; lower heat extends the bake and risks toughening.

glossaryTerms.definition: For knäckebröd, the dough should be rolled until you can almost read through it — roughly 1 to 2 mm. At this thickness, the crispbread bakes dry in around 12 minutes. Thicker pieces remain chewy in the centre; thinner pieces risk burning at the edges before the centre sets. Rolling on baking paper and transferring the paper to the tray avoids the dough tearing as you move it.

content.text: Knäckebröd is a fast, practical bake: no yeast, no rise, no timing to watch. The dough is mixed, rested for 10 minutes to let the rye absorb the water, then rolled to a

and baked at high heat until dry through. The seeds go into the dough, not on top, so they bake into the crispbread rather than falling off. Use caraway for a classic Nordic flavour, or mix sesame and fennel for something milder.

attrs.prepNote: or use sesame, fennel, or a mix

content.text: Heat the oven to 220°C conventional. Mix the rye flour, plain flour, salt, and seeds in a bowl. Add the water and stir until the dough comes together. It will be stiff and slightly sticky. Leave to rest for 10 minutes so the flour absorbs the water fully. Divide into 16 to 18 equal pieces of about 25 g each.

Working one piece at a time, roll directly onto a sheet of baking paper until the dough is 1 to 2 mm thin across a 15 cm round. Use a small round cutter or the lid of a jar to press a central hole if you want the traditional look. Transfer the paper to a baking tray. Bake for 10 to 14 minutes until the edges are dry and lightly browned and the crispbread is set through. It will crisp further as it cools. Move to a rack and repeat with the remaining pieces.

Domestic ovens drift by 20°C or more. An oven thermometer is particularly useful here, since knäckebröd at 240°C burns at the edges before the centre dries. Once cool, store in an airtight tin; they keep well for up to three months.
